hypothesis: The project-switch popover (App.jsx lines 991-1011) is a plain conditional-render div with no click-outside listener and no document-level Escape listener. No window.addEventListener('mousedown', ...) or equivalent hook is registered when the popover opens, so clicks outside never reach any code that calls setProjectSwitchOpen(false). Escape is wired only to the inner <input> onKeyDown — the global keydown useEffect (line 612-622) covers only Cmd+K, Cmd+N, and ',' and has no branch for Escape when projectSwitchOpen is true.

evidence_for.conclusion: App.jsx has zero click-outside detection for any popover. There is no window mousedown listener, no useClickOutside hook, and no onBlur fallback that would close projectSwitchOpen when the user clicks elsewhere.

evidence_for.actual_output: Line 1007 only: inside <input onKeyDown> — if (e.key === 'Escape') setProjectSwitchOpen(false). The window.addEventListener('keydown') useEffect at line 620 handles only Cmd+K, Cmd+N, and ',' — no Escape branch.
evidence_for.conclusion: Escape dismissal is scoped entirely to the input element's own onKeyDown. Once the user's focus leaves the input (which happens naturally when they click outside), a document-level Escape keydown event reaches no handler that closes the popover.
